blogs.title: Day One
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: Yeh a wizard, Arry! Or whatever your name is... welcome, to your first adventure here to the site...
blogs.introduction:
If there was only one thing I could experience again for the first time, it would be to watch that first Harry Potter film, discovering there were already four books released, and going on the adventure into Hogwarts for the first time.

that for the worst game in the franchise, it is still damn fun. Bethesda might have perfected the old video game addage that 'fifteen minutes of fun = success', but there were so many things about Fallout 4 that didn't feel like a Fallout game. Right off the bat, the integration of settlements into the game, ruined the experience very early on. Try as I might those first few times, it felt like a settlement always needed saving right when I was in the middle of killing things. That was the other let down. Creatures that I had once feared and had nightmares about in previous Fallout games, such as Behemoth's and Deathclaw's, were strangely very easy to defeat. And then, I managed to find myself Power Armour, and I literally became too over powered, and that was very early on in the game. My enjoyment skyrocketed, however, upon the discovery of an upgraded Combat Shotgun and then the Railway Rifle. These guns both teared through enemies, so satisfyingly, I'm not even ashamed to say that they were the reason why I went on to finish Fallout 4. It wasn't the sequel to Fallout 3 that I wanted, but Fallout 4 was for the most part fun, which is all you can ask for in a video game.

blogs.title: Day Two
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: Transformers: Fall of Cybertron transports you to the final days of the planet Cybertron where you will experience the darkest hours of the apocalyptic war between the Autobots and Decepticons.
blogs.introduction: From the outset, Transformers: Fall of Cybertron is a spectacular combination of vast eye-catching battlegrounds, stealthy corridors and classy action sequences. This isn’t a bad thing but often the campaign was more exciting to watch than play. Fall of Cybertron starts in a Battlefield 3 style – that is, beginning in the story’s last moments and rewinding to build up the epic confrontation. Throughout the campaign you’ll be swapping viewpoints between the Autobots and Decpticons as a way of testing the player’s allegiance. In the last two hours this feature is used to great effect and everything snowballs in that dramatic final hour. The blend of airborne and ground-focussed missions, as well as the scope of playable Autobots and Decepticons – which have amazing visual presentation – allow for a change in gameplay at just the right moments. The ground vehicles were done pretty well too. It doesn’t matter whether you’re a zippy sports car like Bumblebee or the heavier build of Optimus because it yields better combat in the game. As each Transformer you’ll use a range of detailed weaponry, as well as a special ability too. These include Air Strikes, Grappling, Cloaking and Shockwaves. It was also nice to see that far less ammo was allocated for the heavy weapons rather than the compact ones because when ammo was low, or depleted, you’re encouraged to use vehicle mode instead. Though the campaign was undoubtedly a highlight, the online play was, unfortunately, quite horrible. I was so excited after the campaign but the multiplayer ruined it for me If you’re a Transformers fan, or a lover of action-packed, futuristic shooters, you’ll love this game.

blogs.title: Day Three
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: LA Noire is an action-adventure neo-noir crime game played from a third-person perspective, where the player completes cases — linear scenarios with set objectives — to progress through the story.
blogs.introduction: L.A. Noire provides a picture-perfect depiction of 1940s society in Los Angeles, whilst dealing with its underbelly of crime and corruption. Cole Phelps is a Lieutenant who served in the Marine Corporation during World War II. Phelps is employed by a small police department inside L.A, and the whole game revolves around this one character, with some surprising plot twists. The graphics in L.A. Noire are fantastic, with an emphasis on period vehicles and the art of deconstructing crimes in the different departments. The interrogations, in particular, are visually done well and are a part of crime games we rarely see or are even able to play out. It plays a pivotal part in completing cases and progressing the game, but it's not fool-proof, it is possible to send the wrong guy to jail and lower your rating level. The real winner here, though, is the voice acting: it is flawless. I found the dialogue never lagged behind the character and, with the addition of some colourful language, made those heated moments in the game all the more cinematic. If there are any more video games based around any generation of the police service, they’ll be compared with L.A. Noire. With a solid character base and investigative elements, it’s well worth a look.

blogs.title: Day Four
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: Burnout Paradise is set in the fictional 'Paradise City', an open world in which players create havor by competing in several types of races and challenges, which carries over onto online.
blogs.introduction: Burnout Paradise might just be one of the best, if not the best, arcade racing games I have ever played. The Burnout series, in the past, had been all about crashing and doing damage. With Paradise, the game has evolved into an open-world experience, one that allows you to drive to race locations, or spontaneously go into crash mode and wreak havoc. There are plenty of things to do in Paradise City, things like stunts and collectibles go towards unlocking cars, and when you feel like more, you can fluently jump into an online game. For me, online challenges is what kept me coming back to Paradise. As long as you have a good bunch of players to play with, the hours can melt away. Overall, the mixture of open-world driving, the ability to jump into an event at any time and the online challenges, Burnout Paradise is the perfect arcade racing game.

blogs.title: Day Five
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: God of War is the eighth installment in the franchise overall. Unlike previous installments, this game focuses on Norse mythology and follows an older and more seasoned Kratos and his new son Atreus in the years since God of War III.
blogs.introduction: Kratos, born of Sparta, forged in despair, quenched in blood, and honed in God-hood. Once he stood on the bodies of the God's who played with his life and loved ones, has now gone in to hiding among the pantheon of the Norse realms. He carefully chose the realm specifically to conceal his new love and son, as he tries to learn how to be the husband and father he never got to be in his past life. However tragedy befalls his wife, and she tasks them both with a final quest; for her Ashes to be spread from the highest mountain in Midgard. Thrust from their home, father and son will need to work together to overcome the epic challenges before them, in ways that might not actually be possible. The previous GOW titles were smashem up puzzlers, with combo timers and platforming galore. But in this belt and braces reboot, all of that is cast away in favour of a Castlevania/Rise of Tombraider open world with heavy RPG elements. The map is stunning and vast, combat is crisp and weighty (despite the flighty feeling of the offsider son), and the performances are the best you could ask for. Christopher Judge, Teal'c from Stargate FYI, is flawless as the deeply flawed Kratos. He's turned Kratos from being a 1 dimensional angry rockstar into a genuine and brooding figure, making you feel every twist in his journey in the pit of your stomach.

blogs.title: Day Six
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: Detroit Become Human follows the existence of three androids in a futuristic world. A sentient android hunter Connor, an abused android Kara who escapes her owner to protect a human and Markus, who devotes himself to releasing other androids from servitude.
blogs.introduction: Choice. If I could write a one word review, that would be it. DBH nominally follows the story of 3 androids in the not-too-distant future of once automobile, now automaton Mecca of Detroit. Connor RK800, a police unit assigned to dealing with androids that exhibit 'Deviant' behaviour with partner Detective Hank Anderson, Kara a maid for an unsavoury father and his beleaguered daughter Alice, and Markus a manservant-Cum-Son for world renowned artist Carl Manfred. Choice. The story may follow these broken, confused, heroic characters, however the experience is tied together by one thread: you. The choices, emotional, logical, instinctual, are as much as story driver as a reflection of your own journey being a human. In the style of Heavy Rain and Beyond: Two Souls, it may be unfairly likened to a walking sim with lots of QTE's, but the game play is almost an anachronism to the sheer force of the story telling. There is a dizzying array of choices in each scene, and the replayability is almost endless. This one will look great on your expense report.

blogs.title: Day Seven
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: Assassin's Creed Odyssey recounts a secret mythological history set during the Peloponnesian War, which was fought between the city-states of Greece. The player takes on the role of a mercenary and is able to fight for the Delian League, led by Athens, or the Peloponnesian League, led by Sparta.
blogs.introduction: Assassin's Creed Odyssey continues to evolve by adding new abilities, a lot of new side quest content that adds a good variety to the exploration of the biggest map yet! For the first time you are given a choice between a female or male lead and the conversations you have now give you dialogue options which can impact future events. The game looks stunning and it’s very easy to spend 50-100 hours working your way through all its content. My favourite of the series so far.

blogs.title: Day Eight
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: The scenario centers around Sora's rescue effort of Kairi. Assuming an incorporeal form, Sora travels back in time to the battle between the guardians of light and the Organization, traveling through the guardians' hearts to reach Kairi.
blogs.introduction: KH3 throws you into a magical Disney universe where you play as Sora, a wielder of keyblades, accompanied by Goofy and Donald Duck. There are nine worlds to explore 8 of them being themed with Disney favourites like Pirates of the Caribbean, Toy Story and Frozen. The worlds are beautiful and fun to explore, hiding collectibles that unlock mini games and sneaky hidden Micky symbols to find. The strongest aspect of this game is the battles, discovering new keyblades through your journey which all provide unique special abilities and transformations during battle. You will gain the power to Summon, with characters like Simba providing an epic fire attack, oh and the Attraction Flow, where you jump on theme park rides like the pirate ship that swings back and forward with speed to smash enemies in its path. Every fight is an exciting, fun-filled explosion of animations and colour leaving you wanting more! The story wasn’t so captivating and for those who aren’t interested, they have the option to skip cutscenes and get right back to the action! Let the kid in you have some fun with this one.

blogs.title: Day Nine
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: Set in a post-apocalyptic Oregon two years after a global pandemic, Days Gone follows the story of Deacon St. John, a former member of a biker gang, who embarks on a quest to reunite with his wife.
blogs.introduction: Days Gone Set in the, now eerily prescient, future following a devastating global pandemic that preyed on the elderly and infirm, turning them into hegemonic “freakers” and other assorted type zombies, Days Gone chronicles the struggle to survive of Deacon St John. Army Veteran, Biker Gang Ne’er Do-Well, Widower, Deacon is a no-nonsense ends-justify the means type of guy, which is an ironically refreshing take, much different the morally-tortured morass of anti-heroes that have populated the survivalist genre over the last decade. That is not to say that he is a two-dimensional character in the slightest, but his dilemmas are given vibrancy and visceral weight when he does come across them. On his trusty motorcycle, stopping for petrol where possible, armed with whatever he can find in the ruins of society, Deacon (and old biker buddy Boozer) tries to forget his painful past by doing odd jobs for the other small towns and camps of the surviving diaspora set among the beautifully rugged mountains that typify the Pacific Northwest of the USA. Dynamic weather, including snow and fog, bombastic and subtle characters that fill out the rogues gallery of survivors survivin’, hidden notes and tapes from old research stations and crashed government helicopters, and the plethora of ways to mow down “Hordes” of freakers populating intelligently designed dens, Days Gone is a great way to lose yourself, driving up windy hills, coasting trying to save fuel and not awaken the sleeping freaks too far from home...

blogs.title: Day Ten
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: The story of Final Fantasy X is told by Tidus, a sports star from the futuristic metropolis called Zanarkand. During a match, Tidus gets swept up to another world and joins summoner Yuna and her friends on her journey to save the world of Spira from a rampaging monster known as Sin.
blogs.introduction: I have played a few Final Fantasy titles and this is my most loved so far. You play as a Blitzball player named Tidus who’s city is destroyed by a creature named Sin, you wake up in a distant future and accompany a young priestess Yuna with her friends and guardians on a pilgrimage to destroy Sin. This game has a compelling story and beautiful cinematic clips to go with it. A strategic turn based RPG which will test your skills and make sure you use each character and their unique traits to their full potential to take down whatever crosses your path. I played the original and loved it and loved it just as much going through it again with the remaster. If you have never played FF before this is the one I recommend you try!

blogs.title: Day Eleven
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: As the last remaining BOT-C unit, the game ventures deep into a desert mountain to restore power to an ancient facility, haunted by a desperate Creature.
blogs.introduction: A fantastic little indie pinball-style dungeon crawler about freeing a troubled town from an evil ruler. With a great offering of powerful weapons, character customisation and difficulty, Creature in the Well is equal parts hectic, fun and engrossing.

blogs.title: Day Twelve
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: An island paradise has been discovered by colonial forces from several distinct nations from fictional lands, but with magic and monsters.
blogs.introduction: This tactical fantasy-RPG has you playing as De Sardet, who travels to the island of Teer Fradee in search of a cure for a deadly plague, known as the Malichor. De Sardet can make either allies or enemies of the indigenous people as well as other foreign powers already inhabiting Teer Fradee and the quest for a cure will be more than they bargained for. Players can split skill points into ranged, melee and magic trees. All are surprisingly deep and offer great variations in gameplay, with a 'tactical pause' useful for queuing abilities and making decisions with a more considered approach. It's recommended to invest points into dialogue-based Talents like Charm/Intimidate. This is a diplomacy-heavy title, where convincing political leaders and having the moral backing of party members pays off. Greedfall is by no means a big-budget title but it packs a lot of punch. It's a gorgeous looking game, at times, and being crafted in a colonial-style setting makes it even more eye-catching. Its soundtrack is also top-notch. This is an underrated game worth playing.

blogs.title: Day Thirteen
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: Set in a huge fantasy world, Indivisible tells the story of Ajna, a fearless girl with a rebellious streak who sets out on a quest to save everything she knows from being destroyed.
blogs.introduction: Indivisible is an action role-playing game developed by Lab Zero Games and published by 505 Games. It is a breathtakingly beautiful game, with a hand-drawn art style not to dissimilar to the likes of an anime cartoon. There are a plentiful of unique playable characters to be discovered on your journey, which focuses heavily on platforming and RPG elements. There is a noticeable steady incline of difficulty when attempting to access new areas, hence the continuing unlocking of abilities to further your progress in the game.

blogs.title: Day Fourteen
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: Lost in transit while on a colonist ship bound for the furthest edge of the galaxy, you awake decades later only to find yourself in the midst of a deep conspiracy threatening to destroy the Halcyon colony.
blogs.introduction: The Outer Worlds is, in short, what Fallout 4 should have been. That perfect combination of an open-world game, with incredible story, fun combat and unrivalled character development. It felt more like Fallout than Fallout 4 did; it was a polished, unbuggy and seamless. And, thankfully, there were no shortages of dialogue options to chose from. The Outer Worlds has been described as 'Fallout In Space', and it's not wrong, early in the game you aquire a ship, which results as the games hub and by way of fast-travelling between planets in Hyperion.

blogs.title: Day Fifteen
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order tells an original story about Cal Kestis, a surviving Jedi Padawan, five years after the events of Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith.
blogs.introduction: The Republic lies in smouldering ruins, and the Empire has risen. The Jedi Council and its Jedi Knights are dead or hiding. Their Padawans, deemed just as dangerous by Emperor Palpatine, were wiped out. Save but one, Cal Kestis, our hero, and Only Hope. The stunning opening hour of Fallen Order calls Cal to action to find the true nature of the force, and fight for survival as he is hunted by the Galatic Empire. Across a relatively small number of planets with vast and byzantine maps, Cal combats storm troopers, platforming puzzles, nightsisters and brothers, and giant dragon frogs. Along with BD-1, hands down the best robot sidekick in the extended universe, and the rag-tag lovable crew of the Mantis, Cal faces tests that would turn some Jedi masters to the Dark Side. Combat is intuitive, however the flow is found wanting. When you wish to parry and Yoda-spin around groups of enemies, you find yourself stuck with just one in-between animations, which can jettison you from the experience. However, if you can forgive that, you're in for a treat. Grab some seat kid.

blogs.title: Day Sixteen
blogs.author: IckleRonnikens
blogs.figure: You, experiencing Harry and Hermione for the first time
blogs.summary: Ori and the Will of the Wisps is the sequel that Ori and the Blind Forest deserves, and if possible, the game is even more adorable and heart-wrenching than it's predecessor.
blogs.introduction: The game starts similarly to the first, a thorough opening cutscene that is at first comforting, before complications arise and Ori is yet again forced to save the day. The story in this game alone is enough of a reason for every game lover to experience first-hand. Gameplay is equally fantastic, and seasoned Ori players will immediately familiarise with the system in place, minus the fact that abilities haven't carried over and you have to start over again. That's not a bad thing, slowly building back into a new Ori game is fun and rewarding, with many old abilities mixed in with new ones, to help navigate the world and solve more intricate puzzles. The main differences in gameplay between the old and new Ori, is that the game now relies heavily on autosaving, instead of 'soul links' in the previous title; the sequential upgrade system has been replaced by 'shards', which allows Ori to be upgraded in multiple ways; and then there are the Spirit Trials, a new mode that has players race against the clock across parts of the map to unlock rewards. The final addition to the game are the characters Ori can now interact with. These are allies that are found across the game, that help give Ori useful items such as shards, ability upgrades and maps. They add to the overrall cuteness of the experience and are a nice touch. Presentation, Performance and Story. These are always the three key points in which a game, in my opinion, should be judged. Ori and the Will of the Wisps ticks all the right boxes and more, and except for the occasional framerate and performance issue, the game is a masterpeice.

blogs.summary: Ori and the Will of the Wisps is the sequel that Ori and the Blind Forest deserves, and if possible, the game is even more adorable and heart-wrenching than it's predecessor.
blogs.introduction: The game starts similarly to the first, a thorough opening cutscene that is at first comforting, before complications arise and Ori is yet again forced to save the day. The story in this game alone is enough of a reason for every game lover to experience first-hand. Gameplay is equally fantastic, and seasoned Ori players will immediately familiarise with the system in place, minus the fact that abilities haven't carried over and you have to start over again. That's not a bad thing, slowly building back into a new Ori game is fun and rewarding, with many old abilities mixed in with new ones, to help navigate the world and solve more intricate puzzles. The main differences in gameplay between the old and new Ori, is that the game now relies heavily on autosaving, instead of 'soul links' in the previous title; the sequential upgrade system has been replaced by 'shards', which allows Ori to be upgraded in multiple ways; and then there are the Spirit Trials, a new mode that has players race against the clock across parts of the map to unlock rewards. The final addition to the game are the characters Ori can now interact with. These are allies that are found across the game, that help give Ori useful items such as shards, ability upgrades and maps. They add to the overrall cuteness of the experience and are a nice touch. Presentation, Performance and Story. These are always the three key points in which a game, in my opinion, should be judged. Ori and the Will of the Wisps ticks all the right boxes and more, and except for the occasional framerate and performance issue, the game is a masterpeice.
